title: Birla Mandir, Hyderabad
tagline: Birla Mandir, Hyderabad — spiritual town of Telangana

overview.short: Birla Mandir is a Hindu temple built on a 280 feet (85 m) high hillock called Naubath Pahad 13 acres (53,000 m2) plot in Hyderabad, Telangana, India.
overview.description: Birla Mandir is a Hindu temple built on a 280 feet (85 m) high hillock called Naubath Pahad 13 acres (53,000 m2) plot in Hyderabad, Telangana, India. Naubat Pahad which was once also known as Band Rock so called because long ago all official communications from Mughals to the Nizams were proclaimed from this hill accompanied by music. The construction took ten years and was opened in 1976 by Swami Ranganathananda of Ramakrishna Mission. The temple was constructed by the Birla Foundation, which has also constructed several similar temples across India, all known as Birla Mandir.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Golconda
topPlaces.description: Golconda is a fortified citadel and ruined city located on the western outskirts of Hyderabad, Telangana, India. The fort was originally built by Kakatiya ruler Pratāparudra in the 11th century out of mud walls.

topPlaces.name: Jama Mosque, Golconda
topPlaces.description: The Jama Masjid, originally named as Safa Masjid (Arabic: صفا مسجد), is a Friday mosque located in Golconda, Hyderabad, in the Hyderabad district of the state of Telangana, India. It was constructed in 1518 CE by the first Qutb Shahi ruler, Sultan Quli Qutb Shah, while he was governor of the Bahmani Sultanate in current Telangana.

topPlaces.name: Qutb Shahi tombs
topPlaces.description: The Qutub Shahi tombs are a series of Islamic tombs and mosques built by the various kings of the Qutub Shahi dynasty, located in the Ibrahim Bagh (garden precinct), close to the famous Golconda Fort in Hyderabad, in the Hyderabad district of the state of Telangana, India. A stunning and concentrated example of Indo-Islamic and Qutb Shahi architecture, the galleries of the smaller tombs are of a single storey while the larger ones are two-storied.

topPlaces.name: Masjid E Qutub Shahi Langer Houz
topPlaces.description: Masjid e Qutb Shahi (Arabic: مسجد قطب شاه; Urdu: مسجد قطب شاہی) is a former mosque in a partial ruinous state, located in Langar Houz, Hyderabad, in the Hyderabad district of the state of Telangana, India. The former mosques is associated with the Qutb Shahi dynasty, a ruling dynasty of the region during 1518–1687.

image.alt: Masjid E Qutub Shahi Langer Houz

topPlaces.name: Premamati Mosque
topPlaces.description: The Premamati Mosque (also known as Pema Mati Mosque, Ibrahim Bagh Mosque, or Qutb Shahi Masjid) is a 16th-century mosque located in the Ibrahim Bagh area of Hyderabad, India. Built during the reign of Ibrahim Quli Qutb Shah, it is historically significant as the architectural precursor to the grand Makkah Masjid and for its role in the aborted urban planning of the Golconda Sultanate.

topPlaces.name: Langar Houz
topPlaces.description: Langar Houz is a suburb of Hyderabad, near Golconda, in the Indian state of Telangana. It is a major commercial centre for the city's cantonment area.

topPlaces.name: Shaikpet
topPlaces.description: Shaikpet is located near Tolichowki in Hyderabad, India. It is a mandal in Hyderabad District and one of the oldest suburbs of Hyderabad.

topPlaces.name: Solitaire Global Schools
topPlaces.description: Solitaire Global Schools (commonly abbreviated to SGS) is a group of schools based in Hyderabad, Telangana, India. It was established in June 2017 and has two branches in Hyderabad at Attapur and Kattedan.

items.note: Golconda is a fortified citadel and ruined city located on the western outskirts of Hyderabad, Telangana, India. The fort was originally built by Kakatiya ruler Pratāparudra in the 11th century out of mud walls.

The Jama Masjid, originally named as Safa Masjid (Arabic: صفا مسجد), is a Friday mosque located in Golconda, Hyderabad, in the Hyderabad district of the state of Telangana, India. It was constructed in 1518 CE by the first Qutb Shahi ruler, Sultan Quli Qutb Shah, while he was governor of the Bahmani Sultanate in current Telangana.

The Qutub Shahi tombs are a series of Islamic tombs and mosques built by the various kings of the Qutub Shahi dynasty, located in the Ibrahim Bagh (garden precinct), close to the famous Golconda Fort in Hyderabad, in the Hyderabad district of the state of Telangana, India. A stunning and concentrated example of Indo-Islamic and Qutb Shahi architecture, the galleries of the smaller tombs are of a single storey while the larger ones are two-storied.

items.note: Masjid e Qutb Shahi (Arabic: مسجد قطب شاه; Urdu: مسجد قطب شاہی) is a former mosque in a partial ruinous state, located in Langar Houz, Hyderabad, in the Hyderabad district of the state of Telangana, India. The former mosques is associated with the Qutb Shahi dynasty, a ruling dynasty of the region during 1518–1687.

The Premamati Mosque (also known as Pema Mati Mosque, Ibrahim Bagh Mosque, or Qutb Shahi Masjid) is a 16th-century mosque located in the Ibrahim Bagh area of Hyderabad, India. Built during the reign of Ibrahim Quli Qutb Shah, it is historically significant as the architectural precursor to the grand Makkah Masjid and for its role in the aborted urban planning of the Golconda Sultanate.
Langar Houz is a suburb of Hyderabad, near Golconda, in the Indian state of Telangana. It is a major commercial centre for the city's cantonment area.
Shaikpet is located near Tolichowki in Hyderabad, India. It is a mandal in Hyderabad District and one of the oldest suburbs of Hyderabad.

Solitaire Global Schools (commonly abbreviated to SGS) is a group of schools based in Hyderabad, Telangana, India. It was established in June 2017 and has two branches in Hyderabad at Attapur and Kattedan.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Birla Mandir, Hyderabad?
faq.a: The best time to visit Birla Mandir, Hyderabad is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Birla Mandir, Hyderabad?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (16 km) and the nearest railway station is Kacheguda (Hyderabad) (10 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Birla Mandir, Hyderabad?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Birla Mandir, Hyderabad at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Birla Mandir, Hyderabad?
faq.a: Stays in Birla Mandir, Hyderabad start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Birla Mandir, Hyderabad?
faq.a: The top attractions are Golconda, Jama Mosque, Golconda, Qutb Shahi tombs, Masjid E Qutub Shahi Langer Houz, Premamati Mosque.
